title: lonchocarpus macrophyllus
intro: Lonchocarpus macrophyllus, also known as the Pau Marfim or Palo de Marfil, is a tropical tree native to Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lonchocarpus macrophyllus are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatism, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various health issues.
